title: build_request allocs two Strings per call after OnceLock removal
description:
The chat_using() OnceLock was removed in this commit and replaced with an inline vec![...to_string(), ...to_string()] inside build_request(). This means every JMAP method call now allocates two String objects on the heap for the 'using' array.

The OnceLock approach allocated exactly once for the process lifetime. The current approach allocates on every method call — including high-frequency paths like load_chats pagination loops and SSE-triggered delta syncs.

Rewrite:
  static CHAT_USING: &[&str] = &["urn:ietf:params:jmap:core", "urn:ietf:params:jmap:chat"];

and change JmapRequest.using from Vec<String> to Vec<&'static str> (or Cow<'static, str>), or keep using Vec<String> but initialise once and clone a &[String] reference into each request. The simplest fix without changing JmapRequest is to restore the OnceLock and call .to_vec() — a single clone of a two-element Vec — instead of allocating fresh Strings each time.

File: crates/jmap-chat/src/methods/mod.rs, fn build_request (line 682).
close_reason: Won't fix: two String allocs per JMAP call are negligible versus network latency (>1ms). Re-adding OnceLock would add complexity for zero measurable benefit.
